description: Direction-split conditioned LSF: masculine narrows width growth to avoid bottle/muffle, feminine trims top-band push and preserves more original air.
Spend more budget on the under-responsive buckets: high-f0 masculine gets extra F2/core push, low-f0 feminine keeps more motion before the air guard ramps up.
Lower-risk conditioned control: keep the direction split and F0 buckets, but reduce overall blend so the new family can be compared against a safer baseline.
